steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: Breton's realist style ______ the conventions of Gleyre's romantic style. The passage contrasts the two: realism emphasizes accuracy and ordinary workers, while romanticism uses staged settings and flawless skin. The blank must mean opposed or refused to follow.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [word] needs to express that realism went against romantic conventions. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — rejected: refused to accept or follow — the realist style rejected romanticism's staged settings and idealized skin in favor of accuracy. ✓
  • Choice B — imitated: copied, followed the same approach — the opposite is true; realism deliberately differed from romanticism. ✗
  • Choice C — extended: built on or expanded — this implies realism continued what romanticism started, contradicting the clear contrast in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — epitomized: was the perfect example of — if realism epitomized romantic conventions, it would be the ultimate romantic style, the opposite of what the passage describes. ✗
That is the correct answer.
